title: Today's Markets Brief: 3 Software Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
summary: A look at three software stocks to avoid, the Fed's request for more detail on private credit lending, and my take on the current market.
imageAlt: Image from source article for 3 Software Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
imageCaption: 3 Software Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
imageSourceTitle: 3 Software Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at the latest news in the finance world. Today, I'm focusing on three software stocks that are getting a pass from investors, and a request from the Fed for more information on private credit lending.

First up, we have an article from Yahoo Finance about three software stocks that investors are keeping off their radar. These stocks are OKTA, SOUN, and DBX. According to the article, these companies are reducing operating expenses for businesses, but it seems like that's not enough to get investors excited. My read is that investors are being cautious with their bets on these companies.

Next, the Fed is seeking more detail on banks' private credit lending. This type of lending is described as 'very opaque' by some, and the Fed wants to get a better understanding of the risks involved. This request for more information could be a sign that regulators are getting a little nervous about the lack of transparency in this area.

It's worth noting that the current market environment is quite complex, with many factors at play. From interest rates to earnings expectations, there's a lot to keep track of. For now, it seems like investors are taking a wait-and-see approach with some of these software stocks.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
